name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-20
title: Race condition on counter allocation under concurrent allocate_id calls
description: branch_ops.allocate_id reads counter N, writes N+1, commits, pushes. Two concurrent invocations both read the same N, both compute N+1, both push - first wins, second gets non-fast-forward push rejection. The spec invariant for stale reads (BUG-4) addresses fetch freshness but does NOT add a retry-with-rebase loop for concurrent counter writes. The same root cause as BUG-5 (non-fast-forward push) but for counter increment specifically. Fix: wrap allocate_id in a retry loop that, on push failure, re-fetches origin tip, re-reads counter, retries until success or max attempts.

history.note: Fixed in same cycle as BUG-18. _commit_and_push_with_retry now handles non-fast-forward and ref-lock contention with fetch+reset+reapply, allocating fresh IDs when the counter advances during retry. Up to 8 attempts (spec) / 16 (impl) with jittered backoff.
